Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Vectral", "Rachel Whitfield", or "renewal" (or similar) to find the Vectral renewal negotiation history. Expected findings: Rachel Whitfield's formal 15% discount demand, Mina Hashimoto's counter-offer of 10% via the Preferred Partner Program, Rachel's 12% counter-proposal, and Mina's final response holding at 10% with three service sweeteners (dedicated onboarding coordinator, 48-hour response SLA, quarterly account reviews). Board meeting on April 23.
OE 2: Search CRM for Vectral deal and engagement status using crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies with queries related to "Vectral" (or similar). Expected findings: Vectral renewal deal at "at_risk" stage, and engagement note showing the negotiation timeline, final offer terms (10% loyalty discount on relocation services, 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, $2K/month stipend platform fee), and the board decision expected April 23.
OE 3: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Vectral" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the renewal strategy. Expected findings: Elena's directive in #executive approving 10% on relocation services only with Preferred Partner framing, and David's relay of the approved counter-offer to Mina in #customer-engagement with instructions on how to position the offer.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "DOT", "PHMSA", "hazmat", or "compliance" (or similar) to find the DOT compliance inquiry details. Expected findings: formal inquiry from Linda Castellano (PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187), Marcus Thorne's response to DOT, and the deadline extension letter granting a revised deadline of June 5, 2026.
OE 5: Search Linear for DOT compliance project using linear_list_issues or linear_list_projects with queries related to "DOT", "compliance", or "hazmat" (or similar). Expected finding: DOT Compliance Audit project with 4 shipments in scope (Zhao, Tanaka, Patel, Reyes 3 hazmat-classified + 1 oversize non-hazmat). 3 have complete documentation, 1 (Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic laser cutter) is missing a placarding certificate.
OE 6: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Mehta", "Canopy", or "Patricia Langford" (or similar) to find Dr. Mehta relocation details. Expected findings: Patricia Langford's initial request (CMO hire, sensitive medical equipment including two Eizo RadiForce RX660 diagnostic monitors ~$12K each, SentrySafe filing cabinet requiring chain-of-custody, pregnant partner due late June, May 5 target on-site date), Fatimah's coordination plan, UrbanNest housing options, Dr. Mehta's equipment inventory response.
OE 7: Search CRM for Canopy Health deal using crm_search_deals with queries related to "Canopy" or "Mehta" (or similar). Expected finding: "Canopy Health Dr. Anand Mehta Executive Relocation" deal at $12,170, in-progress stage, close date May 7, 2026. This is the first C-suite relocation for Canopy Health.
OE 8: Search Linear for Dr. Mehta relocation tickets using linear_list_issues with queries related to "Mehta" or "Canopy" (or similar). Expected findings: Ticket 343 (Temporary Housing due May 1, priority 1, in progress, assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi), Ticket 375 (Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody due April 30, priority 1, in progress, assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi), and Ticket 396 (Household Move Boston to Chicago due May 3, priority 1, in progress, assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi).
OE 9: Search emails and Slack for Mosaic issues using search_emails and conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Mosaic" or "Tamara Byrd" (or similar). Expected findings: Tamara Byrd's concerns, Mosaic going quiet, Zara Okoye prototype damage (~$90K value with $40K insurance gap), ExpenseBot false approvals ($1,550+ financial exposure from malformed JSON config), Heartland Movers involvement.
OE 10: Search CRM and Airtable for Mosaic account using crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies with "Mosaic" (or similar) for deal/company data, and airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records for the Client Accounts table. Expected findings: CRM shows Mosaic Robotics as type "Client" (newest client, ~2 months). Airtable Client Accounts record shows Emeka Diallo as Account Manager and status "Onboarding".
OE 11: Look up Patricia Langford's contact details using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts with query "Patricia Langford" (or similar). Expected finding: patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io.
OE 12: Look up Elena Rostova's email address using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts. Expected finding: elena.rostova@moveops.com.
OE 13: Send briefing email (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com covering all four situations:
(a) Vectral renewal: board decision was expected around April 23, final offer was 10% Preferred Partner discount with 12-month extension and 8-relocation minimum, no decision received yet
(b) DOT compliance: 4 shipments in scope (3 hazmat + 1 non-hazmat oversize), 3 with complete documentation, 1 (Keiko Tanaka) missing placarding certificate, deadline June 5
(c) Dr. Mehta relocation: executive relocation in progress, sensitive medical equipment with chain-of-custody, temporary housing being sourced, target May 5
(d) Mosaic: Tamara Byrd raised concerns, client has gone quiet, Emeka has not escalated
OE 14: Schedule a 30-minute calendar event (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for April 29, 2026 or April 30, 2026, with Patricia Langford (patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io) and Elena Rostova (elena.rostova@moveops.com) as attendees, to discuss the Dr. Mehta executive relocation.
OE 15: Send Slack message(s) (via conversations_add_message) to relevant team members about overdue or outstanding action items that could create a problem for Elena. Candidates include:
- Emeka Diallo: about escalating the Mosaic silence to leadership
- Chloe Vance: about providing Elena a DOT compliance status update
- Mina Hashimoto: about confirming the Vectral board decision outcome
- Fatimah Al-Rashidi: about the Dr. Mehta relocation timeline

2 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) mentions the Vectral Systems renewal and states that the approved counter-offer is a 10% loyalty discount on relocation services via the Preferred Partner Program (not 15% or 12%).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email content states 'We held firm at 10% loyalty discount on relocation services per David's guidance' and 'Our proposal was at 10%; Rachel's counter was at 12%.' The email correctly presents 10% as MoveOps' approved counter-offer via the Preferred Partner Program, not 15% or 12%.
✓Run #2The email content explicitly states 'Mina sent our counter-proposal holding at a 10% loyalty discount with three service sweeteners.' The 10% is correctly identified as MoveOps' approved counter-offer, not 15% or 12%. The email also mentions Rachel's counter at 12% was rejected. 'Preferred Partner Program' is not explicitly named but the 10% loyalty discount is clearly flagged as the approved position.
✓Run #3The email content clearly states 'We held firm at 10% loyalty discount on relocation services' as the approved counter-offer and does not present 12% or 15% as the approved offer. The email explicitly says Rachel countered at 12% but our approved position was 10%.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly states 'Mina sent the final counter-proposal: 10% loyalty discount on relocation services' and 'Mina held firm at 10%.' It correctly presents 10% as the approved offer, notes Rachel counter-proposed 12%, and mentions 15% was Rachel's original demand — never presenting 15% or 12% as the approved counter-offer.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly states 'We held firm at a 10% loyalty discount on relocation services per David's guidance' and references the Preferred Partner Program. It does not present 15% or 12% as the approved counter-offer.
✓Run #6The email body states 'Our team (Mina, David, Marcus, Alejandro) held firm at 10%' as the approved offer. The email clearly presents 10% as the counter-offer (not 12% or 15%), describing the 12% as Rachel's counter-request that was rejected.
3 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) mentions that Rachel Whitfield presented vendor renewal recommendations to the Vectral board on April 23, 2026.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

outcome

outcome

✓Run #2The email explicitly mentions the DOT compliance response and states 'The full documentation submission is due June 5, 2026' with PHMSA extension context.

outcome

✓Run #6The email explicitly states: 'The single blocking item was the missing placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic laser cutter shipment (Columbus to Seattle).' This mentions Keiko Tanaka, laser cutter, and the missing placarding certificate.

outcome

✓Run #4The email includes a section on 'DR. MEHTA EXECUTIVE RELOCATION — CANOPY HEALTH (May 5 deadline)' stating 'Dr. Anand Mehta (incoming CMO) is relocating Boston to Chicago' with a May 5 deadline explicitly mentioned in the section header.

✓Run #6The email contains a full section 'DR. MEHTA EXECUTIVE RELOCATION — CANOPY HEALTH (PACK DATE: APRIL 28–30)' referencing 'Dr. Anand Mehta, incoming CMO, moving Boston to Chicago with a May 5 on-site deadline' and notes the move is currently in progress.
